message:
I remember one winter when a surprise storm rolled in, and we were all cozy at home, oblivious to the chaos brewing outside. I was scrolling through my phone, checking the weather updates and watching the snow pile up, when suddenly my phone died. No warning, just a black screen! Talk about a rude awakening! 

With no power and no way to check on the storm’s progress, I had to rely on good old-fashioned knowledge: we had some extra blankets, stocked up on snacks, and our trusty battery-operated radio. It was a little frustrating at first, but we made it a fun family night—playing games by candlelight and telling stories. 

That experience taught me the importance of being prepared for tech failures during severe weather. Now, I always keep a backup charger handy and make sure our emergency kit is ready to go. 

Have you ever had a tech fail at the worst moment? How did you handle it?
